title: Express checks as thresholds, not absolutes
description: Define quality expectations with tolerances calibrated to historical norms so checks catch real regressions without firing on benign variation.
body: Use when defining quality rules on real-world data. Do not assert zero nulls or exact row counts unless the data is truly guaranteed — rigid rules fire constantly and get ignored. Actions: set thresholds from historical distributions (e.g. null rate under 2%, row count within a band), and tighten only where a hard invariant exists (primary key uniqueness). Validation signals include checks that pass on normal data and fail on a seeded regression, with a low false-positive rate. Risk boundary: alerts that cry wolf get muted, so a real incident slips through; calibrate thresholds against history before enabling paging.
